events.title: Toastmasters
events.description:
Who Are We?
We are Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club #1329712.

Our club meets twice a month to practice our public speaking skills in a relaxed, supportive, and fun environment.

All members are encouraged to participate at every meeting. This may be giving a speech, evaluating another club member's speech, or answering an impromptu question.

We strive to provide valuable, effective and supportive feedback that will help all of our members grow as speakers and as confident individuals.

But most important, we have FUN! Anyone is welcome to attend any of our meetings as a guest. In fact, it's encouraged!

We Meet: The 1st and 3rd Monday of EVERY Month at 7:00pm - 8:30pm

Questions?
Email Martha at megorman57@comcast.net mailto:megorman57@comcast.net for more information or visit
Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club (toastmastersclubs.org) https://outermosttoast.toastmastersclubs.org/

events.title: Clams — Past, Present, and Future, with Parker Small
events.description: Once, Truro’s bay teemed with clams, an abundant and essential part of the local landscape and way of life. Today, that population is steadily vanishing.    Join Parker Small—of the family that once owned the historic Highland House resort—for an engaging and timely conversation about the changing ecology of our coastal waters. Drawing on local knowledge and lived experience, Small will explore the role of bivalves in our marine ecosystem and examine how warming ocean temperatures are affecting their survival.    This program invites reflection on both environmental change and the history of Truro’s shores. Part of our free summer series, all are welcome; donations are gratefully appreciated.

events.title: Outer Cape Dance Festival: Movimento Project
events.description:
Join us on Thursday, August 6th for the Movimento Project!

Movimento 2026 will continue the body-mind-movement-art encounter that has populated and fueled our choreographic cauldron since 2015. The Project brings together local movement artists who, now working together for a decade, have supported and developed choreographic creativity that seeks to embody and share steps into our human condition and the movement it might recommend and necessitate. Contemporary choreography moves between and beyond specific dance languages, incorporating "alternative" bodies and diverse perspectives, to reflect on and reveal new and forgotten steps in our own path toward knowing, being, living & celebrating. Our task is choreographic transparency and innovation, connecting to the past while looking at the present and toward the future. We hope to draw our audience, figuratively and literally, into this shared dimension of body movement, conceptually transformed.

Dancing is a metaphor for living life, and as such needs to be explored in all possible spaces. As in past years, the audience will be invited to experience movement in various settings throughout Wellfleet Preservation Hall, from the front yard to the interior spaces and into the back garden.

events.title: Outer Cape Dance Festival: Languages of Nature Workshop
events.description:
In this workshop facilitated by Becky Burrill, we explore the primal and primary language of movement as living perceptual metaphor through improvisational movement-sound games. This is foundation for making mini dances in which each participant is an artistic director of, and participant in, their dance. With this preparation we enter into the very origin of this languaging -- the Natural World. Here, through perceptual metaphor with our surround, each participant makes a dance and teaches it to the rest of us. With all these mini dances we create a dance ceremonial in honor of Nature, accompanied with a musician. No previous experience necessary.

Note: The workshop will begin at Preservation Hall and then move to Uncle Tim’s Island, to create and hold the performance there.

events.title: Josephine Hopper, Also an Artist
events.description: We are pleased to present a compelling evening dedicated to Josephine Hopper—an accomplished and respected artist whose life and work have often been overshadowed by her husband, Edward Hopper.    Bringing together leading voices in Hopper scholarship—Christine McCarthy of the Provincetown Art Association and Museum, Elizabeth Colleary of the Edward Hopper House in Nyack, and the Truro Historical Society’s Helen McNeil-Ashton—this panel will draw on Josephine’s letters, diaries, and an extraordinary collection of her dresses and textiles from our own collection to offer a vivid and nuanced portrait. Together, they will explore the complexities of her artistic career, personal life, and enduring legacy.    This program is part of our free summer series. Due to limited capacity in our exhibition hall, we request you to reserve your seats; donations are warmly welcomed.

events.title: The Mosquito Story Slam
events.description:
Join us on Wednesday, August 12th for The Mosquito Story Slam!

Mosquito Story Slams are an opportunity-for audience members to tell a 5-minute true, personal story based on a theme! Sign up at 6:30pm

The Mosquito is a live storytelling event on the Outer Cape, fueled by YOU, our audience!

Mosquito Story Slams are an opportunity-for audience members to participate by telling a 5-minute true, personal story based on a theme. Stories are told, not read - no notes or props allowed, just you, a mic, and your story!

Our mission is to celebrate and honor the diversity and commonality of our human experiences through the art and craft of live storytelling. The Mosquito has presented thousands of stories to live audiences and podcast subscribers. Each show features special guest storytellers and audience favorites.

events.description:
Join us Thursdays in July and August this summer at 10am for a guided somatic dance experience!

Shake Your Soul is a guided somatic dance practice that energizes your body, relaxes your nervous system, and awakens your soul through a dance repertoire set to world music. Fun, funky, freeing, meditative, graceful, and sweaty, the class invites you to dance from within and with others in community.

This class welcomes all movers and music lovers, and absolutely no dance experience is needed to enjoy this mind-body connection.

About the Instructor: Katy has been teaching expressive movement classes for over 20 years. In addition to Shake Your Soul, she has taught Nia and Zumba to kids and adults.

events.title: 30 Years of Radio Diaries
events.description:
Join us on Friday, August 14th to celebrate 30 years of Radio Diaries! It will be an evening of stories and celebration.

Joe Richman returns to Wellfleet Preservation Hall with Radio Diaries. Join us for the performance and stay for the meet and greet--cocktails and oysters!


Joe Richman Bio

Joe Richman is a Peabody Award-winning producer and reporter and the founder of Radio Diaries, a non-profit organization. For two decades, Radio Diaries has helped to pioneer a model for working with people to document their own lives for public radio. Joe has collaborated with teenagers and octogenarians, prisoners and prison guards, gospel preachers and bra saleswomen, the famous and the unknown. Through his career, he has interviewed hundreds of people, from a seltzer delivery man to a Civil War widow to Nelson Mandela. The LA Times called Joe “a kind of Studs Terkel of the airwaves.”

events.title: Toastmasters
events.description:
Who Are We?
We are Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club #1329712.

Our club meets twice a month to practice our public speaking skills in a relaxed, supportive, and fun environment.

All members are encouraged to participate at every meeting. This may be giving a speech, evaluating another club member's speech, or answering an impromptu question.

We strive to provide valuable, effective and supportive feedback that will help all of our members grow as speakers and as confident individuals.

But most important, we have FUN! Anyone is welcome to attend any of our meetings as a guest. In fact, it's encouraged!

We Meet: The 1st and 3rd Monday of EVERY Month at 7:00pm - 8:30pm

Questions?
Email Martha at megorman57@comcast.net mailto:megorman57@comcast.net for more information or visit
Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club (toastmastersclubs.org) https://outermosttoast.toastmastersclubs.org/

events.title: The Morrill of the Stories: Old & Improved
events.description:
For many years now, Dick Morrill, a local treasure and well-known raconteur, has been wowing audiences at Mosquito Story Slams and elsewhere with his true stories from a lifetime of hilarious and sometimes hair-raising adventures. Like the time he taxied a Boeing 707 through a blinding snowstorm, having had no previous experience doing such a thing even in good weather. And the time he figured out how to drive two cars at the same time. A stroke of genius! And you’ll never forget his completely original take on growing up Catholic.

As an actor, Dick was one of the founders of the Wellfleet Harbor Actors’ Theatre and has appeared on many local stages, including the Academy Playhouse, Cape Cod Repertory Theatre, the Harwich Junior Theater, the Harbor Stage, WHAT for Kids and, of course, WHAT. He was also a founding member of the Narrow Land String Band and will be joined this evening by fellow Narrow Land-er Stephen Russell, on guitar and vocals.

A splendid time is guaranteed for all. All ADULT audiences that is.

events.title: Film Screening — Truro Historical Society’s “Fishing and Fishnet”
events.description: Join us for a special screening of Fishing and Fishnet, the Truro Historical Society’s newest documentary, produced by board member Jan Worthington.    This engaging 42-minute film brings to life the story of John and Tiny Worthington, who played a vital role in Truro’s economic industry from the 1930's to the 1950's through their two businesses: the Pond Village Cold Storage and  Tiny Worthington's Fishnet Industries.    Through storytelling and local history, the film offers a vivid glimpse into an important chapter of Truro's two small businesses during the challenging years of the Great Depression.    Tickets are $10, and seating is limited for this special presentation.

events.title: Zoë Lewis with Roxanne Layton Concert
events.description:
Join us on Friday, August 21st at 7pm for a night of music and storytelling with Zoë Lewis and special guest, Roxanne Layton!

Gypsy swing, latin grooves, vintage jazz played on piano, ukulele and guitar inspired by a lifetime of globetrotting! Zoë's music is like a map, as she recalls stories from her small English village to trips around more that 70 countries. Songwriter and storyteller extraordinaire, she’s performed with the Indigo Girls, Kate Pierson from the B52s, Judy Collins and more! This evening she will be joined by the immensely talented ROXANNE LAYTON on recorder (from Mannheim Steamroller fame). Their boundless energy is hard to ignore. An upbeat and highly original show. Get ready for a dose of joy!

Mike Byrne, Cooper Bennett, and Greg Keating hold down the rhythm section with a rock solid backbeat and supporting vocals. With soaring vocal performances, shredding guitar solos, high-energy drums, and a bassline that you can feel in your soul, Bounce offers a selection of hit songs that everyone can sing along to creating an irresistible urge to sing and dance all through the night!

events.title: Fred Clayton Band
events.description: Fred Clayton was born in 1958 in Birmingham, Alabama. At the age of 11, he moved to Boston, Massachusetts, where he began studying music at the renowned Elma Lewis School of Fine Arts. His early musical foundation continued to grow after moving to Western Massachusetts, where he was influenced and inspired by legendary musicians including Archie Shepp, Marion Brown, Steve McCraven, and Avery Sharpe. Over the years, Clayton has developed a rich musical style shaped by jazz, soul, and rhythm-driven traditions, bringing decades of experience and artistry to every performance. His live shows reflect a deep love of music, blending skillful musicianship with a warm, engaging presence that audiences instantly connect with.

events.title: Derek Dibbern
events.description: Derek’s is a voice that recalls the classic songwriters of the 1970’s. Comparisons to Cat Stevens, Van Morrison and James Taylor come easily, but in addition, he brings an alternative sound reminding one of Wilco’s Jeff Tweedy or Iron and Wine’s Sam Beam. Born among the cornfields of the Great Plains of Nebraska, Derek began playing and writing songs after receiving his first guitar in 1986. Since then, he has travelled widely in the lower 48 playing his original songs and favorite cover versions for audiences from California to New York. As of 2022 he has called Massachusetts his home.

events.title: Summer Jazz Series with Bart Weisman and Friends
events.description:
Admission: $20 at the door (no advance tickets). 



Time: Doors open at 5:30pm, concert begins at 6pm and typically ends by 7:15pm. 







Tonight’s show features Donna Byrne (vocals) with Tim Ray (piano), Marshall Wood (bass), and Bart Weisman (drums).



Bart Weisman performs with his Jazz Group, Klezmer Swing Group, Smooth Jazz Group, and the Cape Cod Jazz Quartet & Trio on Cape Cod, Massachusetts at Events, Parties, Weddings, Concerts, and Restaurants. 



Bart has worked with a number of musicians on Cape Cod including: Molly Ringwald, Jay Geils (J. Geils Band), Ken Peplowski, Rebecca Parris, Joe Muranyi, Eddie Higgins, Lou Colombo, and many more.​ 



Bart is the Founder, Executive Producer & Performer for the Provincetown Jazz Festival, Hosts Jazz Jam Cape Cod, appears at The West End, Bleu Restaurant, The Vine, and Summer Jazz with Bart Weisman Concert Series at PAAM.​ 



Bart is the Jazz Band Director at Sturgis East and directs & performs for the Jazz in the Schools program. 



​Bart relocated with his wife to Cape Cod from Washington, D.C. and was with The United States Air Force Band. Bart has a B.S. degree from the University of Maryland and an A.A. degree in Music from Montgomery College in Maryland.

events.title: The Lustre Kings
events.description: The Lustre Kings hail from Rochester, New York, and have built a reputation as one of the Northeast’s premier rockabilly bands. Inspired by the golden age of American rock ’n’ roll, their sound draws heavily from the spirit of Elvis Presley, Johnny Cash, Carl Perkins, and other pioneers of 1950s rockabilly. With vintage style, slick guitars, driving rhythms, and a high-energy stage show, The Lustre Kings bring the classic sound of early rock ’n’ roll roaring back to life. Their performances are packed with rockabilly favorites, rock ’n’ roll classics, and originals that keep crowds dancing and singing alon g.

events.title: The Mosquito Story Slam
events.description:
Join us on Wednesday, August 26th for The Mosquito Story Slam!

Mosquito Story Slams are an opportunity-for audience members to tell a 5-minute true, personal story based on a theme! Sign up at 6:30pm.

The Mosquito is a live storytelling event on the Outer Cape, fueled by YOU, our audience!

Mosquito Story Slams are an opportunity-for audience members to participate by telling a 5-minute true, personal story based on a theme. Stories are told, not read - no notes or props allowed, just you, a mic, and your story!

Our mission is to celebrate and honor the diversity and commonality of our human experiences through the art and craft of live storytelling. The Mosquito has presented thousands of stories to live audiences and podcast subscribers. Each show features special guest storytellers and audience favorites.

events.title: Shake Your Soul
events.description:
Join us Thursdays in July and August this summer at 10am for a guided somatic dance experience!

Shake Your Soul is a guided somatic dance practice that energizes your body, relaxes your nervous system, and awakens your soul through a dance repertoire set to world music. Fun, funky, freeing, meditative, graceful, and sweaty, the class invites you to dance from within and with others in community.

This class welcomes all movers and music lovers, and absolutely no dance experience is needed to enjoy this mind-body connection.

About the Instructor: Katy has been teaching expressive movement classes for over 20 years. In addition to Shake Your Soul, she has taught Nia and Zumba to kids and adults.

events.title: Material Witness Art Exhibit
events.description:
Join us on Thursday, August 27th at 4pm for Material Witness: An Exhibition by Nancy Marks.

Nancy Marks is a Boston-based artist and social justice advocate who uses her practice to address critical societal issues. She is the co-founder of The Opioid Project: Changing Perceptions Through Art and Storytelling, an initiative dedicated to raising awareness and combating the stigma surrounding the opioid crisis. In 2024, her work was recognized with a First-Place award from the National Organization for Arts in Health (NOAH) for Arts Advancing Social Justice.

Marks’s current traveling mixed-media exhibition, Material Witness, weaves together her mother's experience as a Holocaust survivor with the contemporary struggles of immigrants. The project is also featured in the anthology America’s Slide Towards Authoritarianism (IHRAM Press). This event is free to the public.

events.title: MC Junior
events.description: MC Junior is an Americana artist from Harwich, Massachusetts, blending Outlaw Country, Southern Rock, and Blues into raw, story-driven music. Raised on Cape Cod and introduced to live music at an early age, Junior built his sound on the influence of legends like Waylon Jennings, Willie Nelson, and Alabama. After early success with Junior and the Back Forty — including Massachusetts Country Music Awards nominations for Band of the Year and Male Vocalist of the Year — life shifted his focus to family and recovery. In 2024, Junior returned to songwriting, leading to his upcoming album Broken Bones & Broken Dreams, a record rooted in perseverance, authenticity, and refusing to quit on what matters most. MC Junior’s music is honest, gritty, and true to the Outlaw spirit.

events.title: Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture // Midge Battelle and Pasquale Natale
events.description:
Please join us for a Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture with exhibiting artist Midge Battelle and curator Pasquale Natale.



Love Letter to a Dream will survey work created by Battelle from 1986 to the present day, beginning with hand-printed black and white photographs, moving on to her oil paintings created in the early 2000s, and finally to her more recent work with the Cyanotype medium. 



Included with $15 Museum admission, free for PAAM members. 



THE FREDI SCHIFF LEVIN LECTURE SERIES



The Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture Series welcomes artists, art historians, curators, and authors to speak at PAAM during the summer, both in conjunction with exhibitions and as independent scholars. 



The series was established in honor of artist Fredi Schiff Levin, an active member of Provincetown’s arts community from the 1960s until her passing in 2002. We extend our deepest gratitude to The Levin Family, who graciously sponsors this series. 



Watch all previous lectures our YouTube channel.

events.title: Toastmasters
events.description:
Who Are We?
We are Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club #1329712.

Our club meets twice a month to practice our public speaking skills in a relaxed, supportive, and fun environment.

All members are encouraged to participate at every meeting. This may be giving a speech, evaluating another club member's speech, or answering an impromptu question.

We strive to provide valuable, effective and supportive feedback that will help all of our members grow as speakers and as confident individuals.

But most important, we have FUN! Anyone is welcome to attend any of our meetings as a guest. In fact, it's encouraged!

We Meet: The 1st and 3rd Monday of EVERY Month at 7:00pm - 8:30pm

Questions?
Email Martha at megorman57@comcast.net mailto:megorman57@comcast.net for more information or visit
Outermost Toast Toastmasters Club (toastmastersclubs.org) https://outermosttoast.toastmastersclubs.org/

events.title: Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture // Mary Abell and David Ebony
events.description:
For our final Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture of the season, curator Mary Abell will be joined by writer and journalist David Ebony to discuss the life and work of Fred Garbers.



Included with $15 Museum admission, free for PAAM members. 



THE FREDI SCHIFF LEVIN LECTURE SERIES



The Fredi Schiff Levin Lecture Series welcomes artists, art historians, curators, and authors to speak at PAAM during the summer, both in conjunction with exhibitions and as independent scholars. 



The series was established in honor of artist Fredi Schiff Levin, an active member of Provincetown’s arts community from the 1960s until her passing in 2002. We extend our deepest gratitude to The Levin Family, who graciously sponsors this series. 



Watch all previous lectures our YouTube channel.

events.title: Johnny and the Value Leaders
events.description: Fans of NRBQ, The Spampinato Brothers, and The Incredible Casuals—heads up for The Value Leaders! Delivering high-energy rock ‘n’ roll with a raw, infectious spirit, The Value Leaders bring a sound that’s equal parts classic and electric. With tight rhythms, punchy hooks, and a groove that won’t quit, they keep the good times rolling and the dance floors moving. Whether you’re a die-hard fan of the greats or just looking for some real-deal rock ‘n’ roll, The Value Leaders bring the fire every time they hit the stage.

events.title: Super Soulshine
events.description: Super Soulshine Formed in 2012, Super Soulshine is a Cape Cod–based band known for delivering high-energy performances that blend soulful originals with crowd-favorite covers. With a sound rooted in soul, rock, funk, reggae, and blues, the band creates an upbeat, feel-good atmosphere that keeps audiences engaged from the first note to the last encore. The current lineup features Ron Fuller (lead vocals), Mark Filteau (guitar & vocals), Colton Lange (bass & vocals), and Gino McCloud (drums). Together, they bring tight musicianship, strong harmonies, and a dynamic stage presence to every show. Super Soulshine’s growing catalog of original music — including “Vibes (Have a Good Day),” “Flyin’,” “Today,” “Wake Up,” “I’m Not Alone,” “A Kind Soul,” “Space Cadet,” and “Hot Honey” — highlights the band’s signature groove and uplifting message. Regular performers across Cape Cod, Super Soulshine brings professionalism, versatility, and unmistakable energy to clubs, festivals, and private events alike. Screenshot

events.title: MC Junior
events.description: MC Junior is an Americana artist from Harwich, Massachusetts, blending Outlaw Country, Southern Rock, and Blues into raw, story-driven music. Raised on Cape Cod and introduced to live music at an early age, Junior built his sound on the influence of legends like Waylon Jennings, Willie Nelson, and Alabama. After early success with Junior and the Back Forty — including Massachusetts Country Music Awards nominations for Band of the Year and Male Vocalist of the Year — life shifted his focus to family and recovery. In 2024, Junior returned to songwriting, leading to his upcoming album Broken Bones & Broken Dreams, a record rooted in perseverance, authenticity, and refusing to quit on what matters most. MC Junior’s music is honest, gritty, and true to the Outlaw spirit.

events.title: The Grab Brothers FULL BAND
events.description: Born and raised in Boston, MA, The Grab Brothers Band is: Larry Grab – Bass, Acoustic Guitar, Flute, Harmonica, Sax and VocalsMarc Grab – Electric Guitar, Acoustic Guitar, Trombone and VocalsRob Scott – Keyboards and VocalsTom Corshia – Drums The Grab Brothers are true brothers—Larry and Marc Grab—with music that explodes in a kick-ass blend of “Retro for Today” lead guitar crunching rhythms, mystical-leaning lyrics and arena-filling vocals that pull your heart strings on a whim. But there’s more. These brothers can also strum you a pot-full of acoustical gems and have you singing along to their life-fulfilling stories even when you’ve never heard the songs before. Melora B., journalist for the North-Provincetown Banner recently wrote, “The brothers are like an “orchestrated tossed salad” with music emanating from flute, sax, harmonica, trombone, keyboards and electric and acoustic guitars, which gives them that full jam-band sound. “Their musical heart is enhanced by lead singer Larry Grab who really gobbles up time and verse with an insatiable hunger and passion that is hard to satisfy.” John Thomas of Lip Magazine had this to say: “It’s not just the catchy riffs and the memorable lyrics that distinguish the Grab Brothers Band from others but it’s their musical approach and the way they tell the story with real emotion, understanding and artistry. Larry and Marc can paint you a song about love, life and fantasy and connect you into it as well.” Ultimately, it’s the live performance of The Grab Brothers Band that traps you in their web of musical journeys. This is partially due to their musical theatre background. ​“Their performance and great ability to switch styles in an instant will keep you entertained like your first roller coaster ride,” says Gog at WKKL 90.7-FM radio in Boston, while Tim Emswiller at Noise Magazine adds, “Their sound is like going to a Jethro Tull, Allman Brothers, Grateful Dead and Rush concert with a touch of CSN&Y harmonies put in for good measure!”

events.title: Full Moon Somewhere
events.description: Full Moon Somewhere is a Boston based power trio that performs rock/pop/country/folk with a splash of traditional Irish music throughout the Boston, North Shore and Cape Cod areas. Founding member Barry Hynes(formally of Shoot The Moon) lead vocals and electric/acoustic guitar is well known throughout Boston in Cape Cod. His desire to make music and share the limelight has brought together a group of passionate musicians that can entertain concert, happy hour or dance party events with a versatile repertoire of popular music. Full Moon Somewhere always deliver a melodious passionate high energy performance that will engage all audiences leaving everyone inspired!

events.title: Sarah Swain and the OH! Boys
events.description: Sarah Swain & the Oh Boys have been together since 2009, playing Sarah’s originals, rockabilly classics, and always some unexpected treasures. Their set has a vibrant energy… whether you’re on the dance floor or not -you just can’t help but feel good! They play to packed houses across the Cape, with Sarah on guitar and vocals, Ron Siegel on upright bass, Liam Hogg on drums, and Mark Usher on lead guitar The honesty and enthusiasm of Sarah Swain and The Oh Boys is captured best in their live performances. They have been playing together since 2009 and never fail to get the crowd whipped into a frenzy.!

events.title: Outermost Contra Dance
events.description:
Enjoy live music and dancing with caller Paul Wilde, fiddler Clayton March and pianist
Christopher Marchio.

Doors open at 6:15pm Contra Basic Workshop at 6:30pm. Contra Dance begins at 7pm. Beginners are encouraged to attend. No experience necessary!

We are a friendly bunch, happy to welcome new dancers at any level. Never danced before? No worries, we have, we'll show you how!

events.title: St. Patrick's Day Celebration with Harpist Ainé Minogue
events.description:
Join us March 7th at 3pm for an afternoon of beautiful music from celebrated Celtic harpist, Áine Minogue!

Born and raised in Borrisokane, County Tipperary, Ireland, Áine Minogue is an award-winning harpist, singer, arranger and composer who has entertained Presidents and Prime Ministers, and has traveled extensively to perform at concerts across the United States and around the world.

With a dozen solo album recordings to her credit, Áine has been nominated for several awards for her work as a record producer. Her music, in varying styles including Celtic, World, Folk, Spiritual and acoustic, has appeared on compilations for industry labels such as BMG, Decca, Universal, Sony, Virgin Records, Windham Hill and Putumayo. Many of these albums have topped Billboard Magazine’s World, New Age and Classical Charts for months at a time. In addition, she has completed extensive soundtrack work for PBS, NBC, CBS, ABC, FOX, and MTV. You can find her music on all major streaming services.

Áine Minogue... a balm for our times... BOSTON GLOBE

Áine Minogue... a breath of fresh Éire... ENTERTAINMENT WEEKLY

events.title: Across the Waters
events.description:
Join us on Sunday March 14th at 5pm for Across the Waters: Featuring Susan Lambert and Jessica Georges, with backup vocals and violin by Erica Tso Haidas. Musical Direction by Jonathan Goldberg.

An evocative and eclectic journey through contemporary folk and musical theatre, Across the Waters blends breathtaking harmonies with moments of genuine wit. It is a soulful celebration of art and community, crafted to honor the deep connections that sustain us all.

About the instructor:
Jen Shannon is a certified Kripalu Yoga teacher with over 20 years of experience in Yoga and meditation. In addition to Kripalu Yoga, she has studied and practiced several forms of Yoga including Iyengar, Yin, and Svaroopa Yoga. She has 30 years of experience practicing psychotherapy and mind/body medicine. In her yoga classes she strives to integrate and share her knowledge of health and wellness.
